floodArea.riverOrSea: Bradwell Brook, Dale Brook, Tideswell Brook, Warney Brook and Bentley Brook, Press Brook

items.message:
Forecast rising river levels this evening may lead to flooding. 
Flooding is possible from the evening of 02 May 2026 . 
Areas most at risk are low-lying agricultural land and roads around the tributaries of the Rivers Derwent, Wye and Noe in North Derbyshire including Grinds Brook, Peakshole Water, Bradwell Brook, Stoke Brook, Bar Brook, Tideswell Brook, Warney Brook, Bentley Brook, Via Gellia and Lea Brook. Rainfall is forecast over the next 48 hours. River levels at Perryfoot Farm, Castleton Gauge may rise rapidly due to the nature of the catchment. Levels will remain high until tomorrow morning, 03/05/26. 
We are monitoring rainfall and river levels . 
Avoid using low lying footpaths and any bridges near local watercourses . 
This message will be updated by 9:00AM on 03 May 2026 or as the situation changes.

items.description: Loughborough urban watercourses and local tributaries to the River Soar
items.message:
Forecast rising river levels today may lead to flooding. 
Flooding is possible on 02 May 2026 . 
Areas most at risk are low-lying agricultural land and roads around Whitwick from the Grace Dieu Brook, the Black Brook, Burleigh Brook, Wood Brook, Grammar School Brook, Hermitage Brook, Sileby Brook, Long Whatton Brook, King’s Brook and Kingston Brook. Rainfall is forecast over the next 48 hours. River levels at Whitwick downstream Gauge are rising rapidly due to the nature of the catchment 
We are closely monitoring the situation . 
Take care and avoid walking, cycling or driving through flood water . 
This message will be updated by 9:00AM on 03 May 2026 or as the situation changes.

items.description: River Leen, Day Brook and Tottle Brook in Nottinghamshire

floodArea.riverOrSea: River Leen, Day Brook, Tottle Brook

items.message:
Forecast rising river levels this evening may lead to flooding. 
Flooding is possible from the evening of 02 May 2026 . 
Areas most at risk are low-lying recreational areas and roads including High School playing fields on Valley Road and other low lying land and recreational areas, low lying land and roads around the Tottle Brook. There is a decent likelihood of heavy localised rainfall beginning at 17:00 and continuing overnight and through tomorrow morning. River levels at Day Brook Gauge may rise rapidly due to the nature of the catchment 
We are monitoring rainfall and river levels . 
Avoid low lying roads near rivers, which may be flooded & avoid using low-lying footpaths near local watercourses. 
This message will be updated by 10:00AM on 03 May 2026 or as the situation changes.

items.message:
Forecast rising river levels today may lead to flooding. 
Flooding is possible on 02 May 2026 . 
Areas most at risk are low-lying roads and land around the brooks in Leicester City, including recreational land where water depths could become hazardous.  Areas particularly at risk include those around the Braunstone Brook, Lubbesthorpe Brook, Melton Brook, Saffron Brook, Willow Brook, Bushby Brook, Evington Brook, Thurnby Brook and Scraptoft Brook. Rainfall is forecast over the next 48 hours. River levels at Leicester Braunstone Avenue Gauge may rise rapidly due to the nature of the catchment 
We are closely monitoring the situation . 
Take care and avoid walking, cycling or driving through flood water . 
This message will be updated by 9:00AM on 03 May 2026 or as the situation changes.

items.description: Groundwater flooding in the Great Shefford area
items.message:
Continued high groundwater levels may lead to flooding. Flooding of low-lying areas and roads is possible.

Groundwater levels are falling at our Northfield Farm borehole in the Great Shefford area. Groundwater levels are expected to fall slowly over the coming weeks in response to a period of more settled weather. Due to the nature of groundwater flooding, this situation could continue for several weeks or longer.

We are closely monitoring rainfall and groundwater levels.

Residents are advised to consider property resilience measures and ensure that pumps are working where they have been installed. Keep up to date with groundwater levels on the 'Get Flood Warnings' site on gov.uk.

This message will be updated by 4PM on the 5 May 2026, or as the situation changes.
